articles.title: Rayalaseema sizzles as Anantapur records maximum temperature of 44°C - The Times of India
articles.search_term: ("mercury soars" OR "mercury crosses" OR "temperature soars" OR "record temperature" OR "all-time high" OR "degrees Celsius" OR "maximum temperature" OR "hottest day" OR "temperature crosses 40" OR "temperature crosses 45" OR "temperature likely to rise") Andhra Pradesh
articles.full_text:
Visakhapatnam: Parts of the Rayalaseema region in Andhra Pradesh are reeling under intense heat, with Anantapur recording the maximum temperature of 44.1 degrees Celsius (around 4°C above normal) on Sunday, the highest in the state for the day, the India Meteorological Department (IMD) said.
Along with Anantapur, three other locations in Rayalaseema — Kurnool, Kadapa and Nandyal — occupied the top four spots in the state in terms of the highest maximum temperatures for the day, it said.
Kurnool recorded the maximum temperature of 43.4°C, followed by 42.9°C in Kadapa, 42.6°C in Nandyal and 40.4°C in Tirupati.
In the coastal Andhra Pradesh (CAP) region, Jangamaheswarapuram in Palnadu district recorded the maximum temperature of 40.5°C, followed by 39.6°C in Nandigama, 39°C in Amaravati, 38.7°C in Nellore, 38.5°C in Gannavaram, 37.6°C in Kavali and 37.2°C each in Tuni and Kakinada on Sunday.
The high relative humidity (70 to 80%) has made life difficult in Vizag, with residents reporting discomfort during peak hours on Sunday, even though temperatures ranged between 33°C and 34°C.
The IMD forecast that thunderstorms accompanied by lightning and strong surface wind speeds reaching 30-40 kmph are likely at isolated places over north coastal Andhra Pradesh (NCAP), Yanam and Rayalaseema. Hot and humid conditions are likely to occur at isolated places over coastal Andhra Pradesh and Yanam.
Maximum temperatures are likely to be above normal over the Rayalaseema region on April 20 and 21.
Thunderstorms accompanied by lightning are likely at isolated places over north coastal Andhra Pradesh, Yanam and Rayalaseema. Hot and humid conditions are likely to occur at isolated places over coastal Andhra Pradesh and Yanam on April 22 and 23.
Although the temperature in coastal districts was below 40 degrees Celsius, humidity coupled with heat added to people's woes for the next two days.
Rainfall occurred at one or two places over coastal Andhra Pradesh and Yanam, and dry weather prevailed over Rayalaseema between Saturday morning and Sunday morning, said the weathermen.
The medical and health department suggested people avoid prolonged heat exposure, wear lightweight, light-coloured, loose cotton clothes, cover your head, use a wet cloth, hat or umbrella while going out during peak hours, and drink sufficient water even if not feeling thirsty to avoid dehydration during the summer.

articles.title: Andhra Pradesh Weather Alert: 45°C Heatwave and Thunderstorms to Hit State - Good Morning Andhra
articles.full_text:
The latest Andhra Pradesh Weather Alert has put citizens on high alert as the state braces for extreme weather conditions. Authorities have warned that Monday will witness a mix of intense heatwaves and thunderstorms across different regions, creating a challenging situation for residents. People are advised to stay cautious and follow safety guidelines issued by officials.

Rising Temperatures Under Andhra Pradesh Weather Alert
Under the ongoing Andhra Pradesh Weather Alert, temperatures have soared to alarming levels. According to Prakhar Jain, the Managing Director of the Andhra Pradesh State Disaster Management Authority, the highest temperature recorded was 45.4°C in Nagaradona, Kurnool district. Additionally, temperatures crossed 40°C in 279 mandals, indicating widespread heatwave conditions.
Regions such as Palnadu, Prakasam, Markapuram, and Rayalaseema are expected to continue experiencing high temperatures. Several districts including Srikakulam, Vizianagaram, and Parvathipuram Manyam are likely to face severe heatwave conditions in about 32 mandals. Another 17 mandals across districts like East Godavari, Eluru, and Anakapalli may also witness strong heatwave impact.
Other notable temperature records include 44.5°C in Khajipet, 44.4°C in Karapa, and 44.3°C in Rayadurg and Allagadda. These figures highlight the seriousness of the Andhra Pradesh Weather Alert and the need for immediate precautions.

Thunderstorms Expected Alongside
Despite the intense heat, the Andhra Pradesh Weather Alert also predicts light rains and thunderstorms with lightning in several districts. Areas including Srikakulam, Vizianagaram, Manyam, Alluri, Polavaram, Kurnool, Nandyal, and Anantapur are likely to receive rainfall due to a trough effect.
This contrast in weather conditions makes the Andhra Pradesh Weather Alert even more critical, as people must prepare for both extreme heat and sudden storms. Farmers and cattle herders have been specifically advised not to take shelter under trees during lightning, as it can be dangerous.

Precautions to Follow During
Authorities have urged people to take safety measures seriously during this Andhra Pradesh Weather Alert. Citizens should avoid stepping out during peak heat hours and protect themselves from hot winds. Staying hydrated is crucial, and people are advised to drink plenty of water, lemon juice, buttermilk, and coconut water.
Individuals with health conditions such as heart disease, diabetes, and high blood pressure should be especially cautious. The unpredictable combination of heatwaves and thunderstorms makes it essential for everyone to remain alert and follow official advisories.
The current Andhra Pradesh Weather Alert highlights a rare combination of extreme heat and storm activity. With temperatures rising sharply and thunderstorms expected in several areas, preparedness and awareness are key. Following safety guidelines can help prevent health risks and ensure public safety during these changing weather conditions.
